---
title: "Pelle Shiny App dev notes"
author: "Pelle Tracey"
date: "2022-12-13"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.

# Pelle Notes Start Here

Current project status:

-   Data frames

    -   presentation_murals_2020 = murals with count, zip code, and geographic info for year 2020
    -   presentation_zhvi_2020 = housing values with zip, geo, for 2020
    -   **tidy_yearly = yearly averages by zip**
    -   **murals_long_test = yearly mural counts by zip**

-   Visualizations

    -   Plot 1 = 2020 housing value heatmap
    -   plot 2 and plot 2.5 = 2020 mural count heatmap

-   Goals:

    -   diamond legend style like Felipe suggested does not seem possible. Instead, could do a gradient (graduted colors) for housing values and dots for mural locations. This would require some significant additional data wrangling though, so lets leave it as a "reach" goal.
    -   make shiny maps (2) for each existing visualization




> plot1 = ggplot() +
+     geom_polygon(data = presentation_zhvi_2020, aes(fill = yearly_avg, x = long, y = lat, group = id) , size=0, alpha=0.9) +
+         theme_void() +
+         scale_fill_viridis(trans = 'log10', option = "magma", labels=scales::dollar_format()) +
+         labs(title = "Philly Average Housing Value") +
+         coord_map()
> plot1
> plot1 = ggplot() +
+     geom_polygon(data = presentation_zhvi_2020, aes(fill = yearly_avg, x = long, y = lat, group = id) , size=0, alpha=0.9) +
+         theme_void() +
+         scale_fill_viridis(trans = 'log10', option = "magma", labels=scales::dollar_format()) +
+         labs(title = "Philly Average Housing Value 2020") +
+         coord_map()
> plot1
> plot2 = ggplot() +
+     geom_polygon(data = presentation_murals_2020, aes(fill = mural_count, x = long, y = lat, group = id) , size=0, alpha=0.9) +
+     theme_void() +
+     scale_fill_viridis(trans = 'log10', labels=scales::label_number()) +
+     labs(title = "Philly Mural Concentration 2020") +
+     coord_map()
> plot2
> View(murals_not_on_view)
> View(mural_arts)
> View(murals_long_test)
> View(murals_zip)
> View(tidy_yearly)
> presentation_murals_2020$mural_count[ is.na(presentation_murals_2020$mural_count)] = 0.001
> plot2.5 = ggplot() +
+     geom_polygon(data = presentation_murals_2020, aes(fill = mural_count, x = long, y = lat, group = id) , size=0, alpha=0.9) +
+     theme_void() +
+     scale_fill_viridis(trans = 'log10', labels=scales::label_number()) +
+     labs(title = "Philly Mural Concentration 2020") +
+     coord_map()
> View(plot2.5)
> plot2.5
> presentation_murals_2020$mural_count[presentation_murals_2020$mural_count == 0.001] = 0.1
> plot2.5 = ggplot() +
+     geom_polygon(data = presentation_murals_2020, aes(fill = mural_count, x = long, y = lat, group = id) , size=0, alpha=0.9) +
+     theme_void() +
+     scale_fill_viridis(trans = 'log10', labels=scales::label_number()) +
+     labs(title = "Philly Mural Concentration 2020") +
+     coord_map()
> plot2.5
> plot2.6 = ggplot() +
+     geom_polygon(data = presentation_murals_2020, aes(fill = mural_count, x = long, y = lat, group = id) , size=0, alpha=0.9) +
+     theme_void() +
+     scale_fill_viridis(trans = 'log10', breaks=c(0,5,10,15,25,50) labels=scales::label_number()) +
Error: unexpected symbol in:
"    theme_void() +
    scale_fill_viridis(trans = 'log10', breaks=c(0,5,10,15,25,50) labels"
> plot2.6 = ggplot() +
+     geom_polygon(data = presentation_murals_2020, aes(fill = mural_count, x = long, y = lat, group = id) , size=0, alpha=0.9) +
+     theme_void() +
+     scale_fill_viridis(trans = 'log10', breaks=c(0,5,10,15,25,50), labels=scales::label_number()) +
+     labs(title = "Philly Mural Concentration 2020") +
+     coord_map()
